page: Google Cloud Platform g4-standard-96 is a graphics-optimized server instance designed for workloads requiring dedicated hardware acceleration. Built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, this instance features 96 dedicated virtual CPUs and 360.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 3.75 GB per vCPU.

The primary hardware differentiator for the g4-standard-96 is the inclusion of two bundled NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000 GPUs. This configuration does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached block storage options. Additionally, the instance provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the high density of dedicated vCPUs combined with dual professional-grade GPUs, this instance is structured for parallel processing tasks. It is qualitatively positioned as a high-resource tier where cost efficiency is realized through dense GPU-to-CPU resource allocation rather than low-cost entry points. Appropriate workloads include GPU-accelerated rendering, 3D modeling, machine learning inference, and complex visualization tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform g4-standard-96 is a graphics-optimized server instance designed for hardware-accelerated workloads. It features 96 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 360.0 GB of system memory, establishing a resource ratio of 3.75 GB of RAM per core. The core capability of this instance is driven by two bundled NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000 GPUs, making it highly suitable for parallel computing tasks. This configuration does not include local storage, requiring external network-attached storage solutions for data persistence. It also provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The g4-standard-96 is qualitatively optimized for resource-dense workloads where dual-GPU acceleration and high vCPU counts are required, such as 3D rendering, visualization, and machine learning inference.
